steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that other economists "clearly find her studies valuable" (after the dash), and gives frequent citations as evidence. The blank must connect the citations to that conclusion — the word should mean "emphasizes" or "supports."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — forestalls: means to prevent or pre-empt. Frequent citations don't prevent anything; this goes the wrong direction. ✗
  • Choice B — belies: means to contradict. The passage argues for her usefulness, not against it. ✗
  • Choice C — underscores: means to emphasize or highlight. Frequent citations underscore (i.e., reinforce) the conclusion that other economists value her work. ✓
  • Choice D — overshadows: means to diminish by comparison. This would imply citations reduce rather than support her usefulness. ✗
That matches Choice C — underscores.
